title: Sour Nostalgic Venezuelan Pasta Empanadas
description: A twist on traditional empanadas, incorporating pasta water, coconut flakes, and a hint of sourness.
instructions: Cook the pasta according to the package instructions until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water and then drain the pasta.
In a large skillet, heat the oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the sour cream to the skillet, stirring to combine with the onion and garlic mixture.
Reduce heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the Parmesan cheese to the mixture and stir until melted.
Add the reserved pasta water to the mixture, stirring to combine.
In a separate bowl, mix together the flour, coconut flakes, and a pinch of salt.
Gradually add the dry mixture to the pasta water mixture, stirring to combine.
Add the cooked pasta to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Using a cookie scoop or your hands, shape the mixture into 8-10 empanada shapes.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with a little water and sprinkle with additional coconut flakes if desired.
